---
title: "Out-of-Balance Disc"
format:
#   html:
#     toc: true
#     number-sections: true
#     theme: cosmo
#     code-fold: true
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    geometry: margin=2.5cm
---

## Example

Two identical holes are drilled in a uniform circular disc at a radius of **400 mm** from the axis. The total mass of material removed is **187.5 g**. The holes are spaced **90° apart** and the disc rotates at **1000 rpm**.

1.  Calculate the resultant out-of-balance force.
2.  Determine the magnitude and angular position of a corrective mass placed at a radius of **250 mm** to fully balance the disc.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Given Data

| Symbol           | Description              | Value               |
|------------------|--------------------------|---------------------|
| $r$              | Hole radius from axis    | 400 mm = 0.4 m      |
| $m_\text{total}$ | Total mass removed       | 187.5 g = 0.1875 kg |
| $\theta$         | Angular spacing of holes | 90°                 |
| $N$              | Rotational speed         | 1000 rpm            |
| $r_b$            | Balancing mass radius    | 250 mm = 0.25 m     |

Each hole represents an equal share of the removed material:

$$m = \frac{m_\text{total}}{2} = \frac{0.1875}{2} = 0.09375 \ \text{kg}$$

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Angular Velocity

$$\omega = \frac{2\pi N}{60} = \frac{2\pi \times 1000}{60} = 104.72 \ \text{rad/s}$$

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Resultant Out-of-Balance

### Mass–Radius Vectors

Removing material creates an effective **unbalance** equivalent to two negative masses. Place hole 1 at 0° and hole 2 at 90°.

$$\vec{u}_1 = mr\,\hat{e}_{0°}
  = 0.09375 \times 0.4 \ \text{kg·m at } 0°
  = 0.0375 \ \text{kg·m at } 0°$$

$$\vec{u}_2 = mr\,\hat{e}_{90°}
  = 0.09375 \times 0.4 \ \text{kg·m at } 90°
  = 0.0375 \ \text{kg·m at } 90°$$

### Vector Sum (Cartesian components)

$$U_x = 0.0375\cos 0° + 0.0375\cos 90° = 0.0375 + 0 = 0.0375 \ \text{kg·m}$$

$$U_y = 0.0375\sin 0° + 0.0375\sin 90° = 0 + 0.0375 = 0.0375 \ \text{kg·m}$$

### Resultant Magnitude

$$U = \sqrt{U_x^2 + U_y^2}
    = \sqrt{0.0375^2 + 0.0375^2}
    = 0.0375\sqrt{2}
    = 0.05303 \ \text{kg·m}$$

### Direction

The resultant bisects the 90° angle between the two holes:

$$\phi = \arctan\!\left(\frac{U_y}{U_x}\right)
       = \arctan(1) = 45° \text{ from hole 1}$$

### Out-of-Balance Force

$$\boxed{F = U\,\omega^2 = 0.05303 \times 104.72^2 \approx 581 \ \text{N}}$$

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Balancing Mass

To restore equilibrium the corrective mass must produce an equal and **opposite** $mr$ vector — i.e. at **225° from hole 1** (45° + 180°).

$$m_b \, r_b = U \implies m_b = \frac{U}{r_b} = \frac{0.05303}{0.25}$$

$$\boxed{m_b = 0.2121 \ \text{kg} \approx 212 \ \text{g}}$$

Placed at a radius of **250 mm** and at **225° from hole 1** (i.e. diametrically opposite the resultant unbalance vector).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Summary

| Quantity                        | Value                         |
|---------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| Angular velocity $\omega$       | 104.72 rad/s                  |
| Resultant unbalance $U = mr$    | 0.05303 kg·m                  |
| Direction of unbalance          | 45° from hole 1               |
| Out-of-balance force $F$        | **581 N**                     |
| Corrective mass $m_b$ at 250 mm | **212 g at 225° from hole 1** |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Verification (Python)

```python
import numpy as np

# Given
m_each   = 0.1875 / 2          # kg, mass per hole
r        = 0.400                # m, hole radius
N        = 1000                 # rpm
r_b      = 0.250                # m, balance radius
theta    = np.radians(90)       # hole spacing

# Angular velocity
omega = 2 * np.pi * N / 60
print(f"ω = {omega:.4f} rad/s")

# mr vectors
u1 = m_each * r * np.array([np.cos(0),     np.sin(0)])
u2 = m_each * r * np.array([np.cos(theta), np.sin(theta)])

U_vec = u1 + u2
U_mag = np.linalg.norm(U_vec)
U_angle = np.degrees(np.arctan2(U_vec[1], U_vec[0]))
print(f"Resultant mr = {U_mag:.5f} kg·m at {U_angle:.1f}°")

# Out-of-balance force
F = U_mag * omega**2
print(f"Out-of-balance force F = {F:.1f} N")

# Balancing mass
m_b = U_mag / r_b
balance_angle = U_angle + 180
print(f"Corrective mass = {m_b*1000:.1f} g at {balance_angle:.1f}° from hole 1")
```
